mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, consumer spending power deteriorates and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust demand without monetary policy tightening pressure, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals immediate capacity utilization improvements and short-term earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns in recovery periods, or the predictive power vanishes when GDP growth exceeds 3.0 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 20-day forward returns in >50% of observations, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.1 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction demand and related supply chains, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of periods when HOUST drops below the threshold, or the signal shows no predictive edge in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation alongside a rising federal funds rate indicates aggressive monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations and reduces forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined inflation and rate increase signal yields positive returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero in multivariate models.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during stable policy environments.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above threshold fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or the mechanism reverses direction when monetary policy shifts to aggressive tightening.
